prompt: Where should `PostgresClient(...)` actually be constructed in a Spring application — inside the service class, inside the repository class, inside a @Configuration class, or somewhere else? Explain via dependency rules.
rubric: States that the domain (service) must not name PostgresClient directly
Distinguishes domain-owned interface (e.g., ReportGateway / repository abstraction) from infrastructure implementation
Places `new PostgresClient(...)` at the composition root (in Spring: @Configuration / @Bean method)
Mentions 'crossing the curve' or the directional inversion (source-code deps cross toward abstraction; control flow crosses back at runtime)
